page: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and network interfaces with a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with the stress-ng multi-core test and Passmark CPU score placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read and write bandwidth, reaching 550.79 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Cryptographic and vector execution capabilities are supported by hardware flags such as AVX-512 and AES, which contribute to top-tier scores in Passmark encryption and extended instruction tests. However, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform at average levels.

This instance is configured for workloads requiring high memory bandwidth and massive parallel CPU capacity without local storage requirements. It is suited for Redis database operations, where it achieves 251.75 million operations per second, as well as software compilation and LLM prompt processing. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning storage and external routing must be provisioned separately.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose server utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz, paired with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs, but provides a 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.32xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and cryptographic execution, alongside strong database and software compilation capabilities. However, single-threaded gzip compression and large-model text generation speeds remain in the average tier. This server is suited for memory-bandwidth-sensitive workloads, Redis deployments, and parallelized CPU tasks such as software compilation, though users must account for the lack of local storage and complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 128 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.32xlarge features 128 AMD EPYC cores, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network speed for high-throughput parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-core-density general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for parallelized, memory-bandwidth-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz.
512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory.
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark results.
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration.
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware.
Suited for Redis operations, software compilation, and parallel CPU tasks.
